title: Identifier support and rarity features
summary: Add frequency and rare-context support features for Driver, Race, Race_Year, and Driver_RaceYear without changing categories in 001214.
rationale: This patch uses ID support counts to regularize brittle memorization. This patch tests the numeric support signal only, avoiding rare-token replacement as a separate confound.
implementation_hint: Start from 001214 exactly. Add only these support-count numeric features: for `Driver` threshold 8, `Race_Year` threshold 12, and `Race` threshold 12, compute `freq = value_counts[col]`, add `{col}_freq = freq`, `{col}_log_freq = log1p(freq)`, `{col}_is_rare = freq <= threshold`. Also compute `Driver_RaceYear = Driver + '__' + Race_Year`, `Driver_RaceYear_freq = value_counts[Driver_RaceYear]`, `Driver_RaceYear_log_freq = log1p(Driver_RaceYear_freq)`, `Driver_RaceYear_is_rare = Driver_RaceYear_freq <= 3`. Important: do not replace `Driver`, `Race`, or `Race_Year` with `RARE_*` tokens in this patch; only add numeric/count features. Do not add deployment-likeness, service-window, or class-aware margin features.
expected_effect: Improve robustness in sparse driver/race contexts while preserving the parent model's existing category handling.
risk: Counts computed within each preprocessed split may encode fold composition; if the parent already has count encodings, this can be redundant.
